title: Image 2 of The daily union (Washington [D.C.]), December 22, 1854
description: Mr TAYLOR of Tennewee wished to know whether the bill had been read The CHAIRMAN Mr Bocock attid that the bill as now reported from the Committee on the Judiciary had not been read Mr TAYLOR of Tennessee thought it unusual to force a bill through the House without being read and moved that the House adjourn which was not agreed toyeas 40nays 76 The amendments of the committee were then reported and agreed to Neve al motions were then made to reconsider lay on the tuble and to adjourn all of which were cither rejected or ruled out of order by the Chair Mr HAMILTON moved the previous question on ordering the hill to he read a third time and it wits ordered On motion the House adjourned emrioNs kt By Mr FENTON The petition of citixeni of Clinton countv New York for bounty land to soldiers who served in the war of 1C12 uud for a revision of our bountyland and pen inn laws referred to the Committee on Pu6lic Lands Also notice of his intention to introduce on toinorrow or some subsequent day a bill for continuing the improve I meat of the harbor of Dunkirk New
